Gameplay Eras Pricing FAQ Account Play free

Can you win the ratings war?

Build radio stations. Fight for listeners. Crush the competition.

Play free now

No signup. No download. Start playing instantly.

Sign in — full game How it works

Best on tablet or desktop, but playable in your browser.

Launch Stations

Pick formats, build brands, and decide whether to chase mass appeal or carve out a niche.

Hire Talent

Build lineups that win listeners — and decide when expensive stars are worth the risk.

Compete for Ratings

Watch rivals react, markets shift, and revenue rise or collapse with your decisions.

Evolve with the Industry

Adapt as AM fades, FM takes over, consolidation changes the business, and new eras rewrite strategy.

Gameplay

Run the Dial. Own the Market.

Every turn forces tradeoffs: format choices, talent costs, branding, programming, debt, and market timing. Build a winner — or watch a once-great station fade.

Compete in Real Markets

Battle through markets like Los Angeles, Seattle, Atlanta, or Nashville and see how strategy changes by city.

Build Winning Stations

Shape station identity, choose formats, and manage the lineup that drives ratings and revenue.

Live with the Consequences

Great ratings do not guarantee survival. Costs, competition, and timing can wreck a station fast.

BRING YOUR STATION TO LIFE

Generate your own station logos and custom promotional vans and mobile units—so the brands you build on the air also look and feel alive on the street.

Sample jingle (tap play)

Jingles you can hear

Produce your own station jingles and listen to your station’s sound—tight, era-appropriate IDs that make every format feel like a real signal on the dial.

HOW FAR CAN YOU GO?

Start your career in Wichita and see how high up the corporate ladder you can climb—earn moves, prove yourself, and take on bigger markets as the company grows.

The Twist

Radio Changes. You Adapt.

No two games are the same. These are living, breathing markets. What works in 1973 may fail by 1983. The audience and business are changing all the time.

1970s

AM still matters

Top 40 stations battle each other for young ears, old-line AM stations still get big audiences.

1980s

FM Takes Over

Cars get FM radios and that’s where people want to hear music. How will AM stay relevant?

1990s

Radio Consolidates

Ownership rules change, clusters rise, strategy becomes more ruthless.

2000s+

New pressures

More stations, streaming audio, changing ad markets and shifting audience habits force hard choices.

What players say

Follow the development of Airwave Empire

Get occasional updates

Get occasional updates, strategy notes, and announcements about new markets and features. New markets and major features are added regularly!

No spam. Usually 1–2 updates per week.

FAQ

Questions

Do I need to know radio to play?

No. If you like strategy, management, and competition, you can play. Radio knowledge helps, but it is not required.

Is this single-player or multiplayer?

Both. Solo is always available. Multiplayer works on supported plans (see Pricing). On the free tier you can host multiplayer only in Atlanta, but you can still join a subscriber’s room in other cities with a room code.

What’s the difference between the free trial, Starter, and Pro?

Your first full solo game is free with full access. After that run, subscribe to start additional games, or keep playing for free in the Atlanta market only — compare Starter and Pro above.

Do I need to download anything?

No. Airwave Empire runs right in your browser.

Can I play on my phone?

You can open the game on a phone, but the interface is built for desktop, laptop, and tablet screens. For the best experience—and to avoid a lot of scrolling and zooming—use a larger display.
